title: Multilane Asymmetric Exclusion Process with stationary Bernoulli measure
abstract: We consider an Asymmetric Exclusion Process evolving on parallel mutually interacting lanes with neighbouring nearest hoppings of hardcore particles. Number of particles on each lane is conserved. We find a choice of the hopping rates, for which the process has Bernouilli stationary product measure, and calculate the stationary particle currents as a function of average particle densities.
summary: This paper identifies the specific hopping rate conditions required for a multilane ASEP to maintain a simple Bernoulli stationary state. It provides a generalized formula for particle currents that remains valid even for negative interaction constants, extending previous results beyond uni-directional hopping.
main_problem: Finding the sufficient conditions for hopping rates in a multilane Asymmetric Exclusion Process (ASEP) to yield a stationary Bernoulli product measure and providing a stochastic interpretation for interaction constants across the full real axis.
main_result: The author derives a specific condition for the difference between forward and backward hopping rates and provides a generalized formula for the stationary particle current as a function of average particle densities.
method: The study uses the stationary Master equation and an algebraic approach to linearize conditions, employing an iterative summation procedure to extend the solution from two lanes to K lanes.
model_or_system: A multilane Asymmetric Exclusion Process (ASEP) consisting of K parallel, mutually interacting lanes where particles follow the exclusion principle and hopping rates depend on the occupation of neighboring lanes.
key_observables: Stationary particle currents (j_alpha) and average particle densities (rho_alpha).
important_parameters: Hopping rates (r, r', s, s'), interaction constants (gamma_alpha_mu), and the infinite lattice limit.
main_assumptions: The system is in the infinite lattice limit with a Bernoulli stationary state (zero correlation length) and lane changes are forbidden.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the multilane ASEP setup and hopping rate dependence; Figure 2 illustrates the notation for a two-lane ASEP model.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the multilane ASEP model, solves the two-lane case to identify necessary rate relationships, uses iterative summation to generalize to K lanes, and concludes with the derivation of the current formula.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic setup of a Multilane ASEP model. Each particle hops to nearest neighbouring sites on the same lane if it is empty. Rates of selected hoppings, shown by arrows, depend on the configuration of particles within the rectangle on all the lanes. For Bernoullli measure to be stationary the rates should satisfy the condition (10), see text.
FIG. 2. Schematic setup of a two-lane ASEP model with hopping rates r(d), r′(d) and s(d), s′(d), see text.
